title: 10 petabytes of sensitive data stolen from China's National Supercomputing Center, hackers claim daring heist would be largest ever China hack, covering 6,000 clients across science, defense, and beyond
summary: Hackers stole 10 petabytes of sensitive data from China's National Supercomputing Center, compromising aerospace, bioinformatics, and fusion modeling secrets. The data breach affects 6,000 clients across various fields. This daring heist is claimed to be the largest ever China hack.
insight: Global security and innovation compromised by massive data breach.

title: Intel and SambaNova team up on heterogenous AI inference platform different hardware performs different workloads
summary: Intel and SambaNova collaborate on a heterogeneous AI inference platform that combines Intel Xeon 6 CPUs, SambaNova SN50 RDUs, and Nvidia GPUs to optimize workload performance.
insight: Different hardware optimizes various AI workloads for improved efficiency.

title: PCI Express roadmap: The path to 1TB/s with PCI 8.0, the challenges of integration, and beyond
summary: The PCI Express roadmap is set to reach 1TB/s with the introduction of PCIe 8.0, but manufacturers face significant integration challenges. The future of PCIe technology will rely on advancements in materials and design. As the industry moves beyond PCIe 6.0, new innovations will be necessary to maintain performance and efficiency.
insight: PCIe's scalability is crucial for future computing and storage needs.

title: AMD reveals $899 price tag for Ryzen 9 9950X3D2 first dual-cache X3D CPU is $200 more expensive than the Ryzen 9 9950X3D
summary: AMD's Ryzen 9 9950X3D2 launches at $899, a $200 price increase from the previous model.
insight: Price hike may impact consumer purchasing decisions and AMD's market competitiveness.

title: Bain Capital's data center unit removes disgraced tenant suspected of smuggling Nvidia GPUs to China Megaspeed previously alleged to have spent roughly $2 billion on AI processors for illicit distribution
summary: Bain Capital's Bridge Data Centers cancels Megaspeed's tenancy and replaces it with Zenplayer. Megaspeed was previously accused of smuggling Nvidia GPUs to China. The move is seen as a major blow to the company's operations.
insight: U.S. companies are taking a stand against illicit AI technology exports.

title: British cryptographer Adam Back is the secret creator of Bitcoin, claims new report Back refutes investigation, says parallels to Satoshi are just a coincidence
summary: A New York Times investigation suggests Adam Back is Satoshi Nakamoto, but he denies it, citing coincidence and confirmation bias.
insight: Adam Back's possible connection to Satoshi raises questions about Bitcoin's true origins.

title: Asus ROG Xbox Ally review: The cheapest Windows handheld gets points for showing up
summary: The Asus ROG Xbox Ally is the cheapest Windows handheld, but it's held back by technical issues and a mediocre processor, making it less competitive than the Steam Deck OLED.
insight: Affordability comes at the cost of performance and features.

title: A brief history of Denuvo DRM and the new hypervisor bypass inside the cat-and-mouse game between Denuvo and the piracy scene
summary: Denuvo DRM emerged as a powerful anti-piracy tool in 2014, initially effective at protecting AAA titles like Dragon Age: Inquisition. However, the cat-and-mouse game between Denuvo and the piracy scene led to numerous bypasses, including the recent discovery of a hypervisor bypass. This has raised questions about the effectiveness of Denuvo in the face of relentless piracy efforts.
insight: Piracy scene's adaptability undermines anti-piracy software's long-term effectiveness.

title: Taiwanese chip makers call on government to stockpile helium, liquid natural gas TSIA pleads for strategic supplies as US and Iran sign ceasefire in Middle East
summary: Taiwan's semiconductor industry alliance urges the government to stockpile helium and LNG to ensure stability during crises, citing the need for alternative suppliers and restarting nuclear power plants.
insight: Semiconductor supply chain resilience hinges on strategic gas stockpiling.

title: Get $100 off the ROG Xbox Ally handheld Ryzen Z2 A-powered version with 16GB of RAM is under $500 again
summary: Get the ROG Xbox Ally handheld with 16GB RAM for under $500, featuring a 120Hz display powered by AMD's Ryzen Z2 A chip.
insight: Gaming on-the-go just got significantly more affordable for enthusiasts.

title: Snap up 32GB of Corsair Vengeance RAM for just $192 when you pair it with AMD's 9800X3D processor and Asus X870E motherboard for $1,054 bundle also includes a free 240mm AIO cooler and a copy of Crimson Desert
summary: Get a high-performance PC bundle featuring 32GB Corsair Vengeance RAM, AMD 9800X3D, and a free AIO cooler, along with a copy of Crimson Desert, all for $1054.98.
insight: High-end components bundled at a competitive price point for enthusiasts.

title: Russian state hackers are hijacking TP-Link and MicroTik routers to steal Outlook credentials, cybersecurity center warns APT28 group targets DNS and redirects traffic to attacker-controlled servers
summary: Russian state hackers are exploiting vulnerable routers to steal Outlook credentials. They target DHCP and DNS settings on small office and home office routers. This allows them to redirect traffic to attacker-controlled servers.
insight: Russian state hackers exploit vulnerable routers to steal sensitive credentials

title: be quiet! Pure Power 13 M 1200W supply review: Platinum-level efficiency, premium pricing
summary: The Be Quiet! Pure Power 13 M 1200W offers exceptional build quality, Platinum-level efficiency, and a comprehensive 10-year warranty, but its premium pricing and limited connectivity are notable drawbacks.
insight: High-end power supply with premium features at a high price point.

title: China intensifies efforts to poach semiconductor talent from Taiwan, claims report international restrictions motivate illicit efforts to obtain talent and equipment
summary: China is allegedly increasing efforts to steal Taiwan's semiconductor technologies and know-how amid international restrictions.
insight: China's chip tech heists threaten global semiconductor supply chain stability.

title: Corsair's new customizer tool lets buyers trick out the Frame 4000D case configurator lets you explore every possible option for this highly modular enclosure
summary: Corsair's new case customizer lets buyers personalize the Frame 4000D case with a wide range of options, from side panels to front-panel I/O configurations. This allows for highly modular and tailored enclosures that meet individual needs. The customizer tool makes it easy to explore every possible option.
insight: Endless customization options for highly modular and tailored enclosures.

title: Sipeed T256s A USB thermal camera with 256 192 LWIR sensor, 640 480 AI super resolution
summary: The Sipeed T256s is a portable USB thermal camera with a 256x192 LWIR sensor and AI-powered super-resolution up to 640x480 resolution. It features a 1.69-inch touchscreen, macro lens, internal storage, and durable CNC aluminum enclosure for efficient heat dissipation. It is designed for electronics R&D, industrial maintenance, and HVAC diagnostics.
insight: Real-time thermal imaging with AI-powered super-resolution enhances accuracy in diagnostics

title: Flash Bee An ESP32-C3-based DIY handheld lightning detector
summary: The Flash Bee is a DIY handheld lightning detector made with off-the-shelf components, utilizing the AMS AS3935 sensor to detect lightning up to 40 km away with 1km accuracy. It features a Seeed Studio XIAO ESP32-C3 board, a Round Display for XIAO, and a 3D-printed enclosure. The design is easy to reproduce and convenient to use.
insight: Detects lightning up to 40 km away with 1km accuracy.

title: WeAct STM32U585CIU6 Core Mini An $8 STM32U5 board supported by MicroPython v1.28
summary: The WeAct STM32U585CIU6 Core Mini is a low-cost, standalone STM32U5 MCU board priced at $8, supported by MicroPython v1.28. It features a ST STM32U585CIU6 Core Arm Cortex-M33 microcontroller with up to 160 MHz clock speed and 2048 KB flash memory.
insight: Low-cost STM32U5 board expands MCU options for makers and hobbyists worldwide.

title: NASA shortens key radiation shielding demo on Artemis II flight
summary: NASA has shortened a key radiation shielding demo on Artemis II due to time constraints. The decision was made to ensure the crew could prepare for Earth entry on Friday. This demo tested shielding materials for future lunar missions.
insight: Radiation protection is crucial for long-term lunar space travel

title: ChromeOS Flex gets a $3 USB installer to revive aging Windows PCs
summary: Google partners with Back Market to offer ChromeOS Flex as a $3 USB installer, enabling users to revive aging Windows PCs and extend their lifespan. This partnership aims to promote ChromeOS Flex as a secure, sustainable, and fully supported alternative. The affordable installer makes ChromeOS Flex accessible to a wider audience.
insight: Revamping outdated PCs with sustainable, secure ChromeOS Flex alternatives

title: Snapdragon X2 laptops launch with up to 18 cores, 5.0GHz clocks, and 80 TOPS NPU
summary: Qualcomm's Snapdragon X2 laptops offer up to 18 cores, 5.0GHz clocks, and 80 TOPS NPU, targeting demanding workloads.
insight: Revolutionizing high-performance computing with massive processing capabilities.

title: CIA deployed secret "Ghost Murmur" AI to track down missing airman in Iran
summary: The CIA allegedly deployed a secret AI called Ghost Murmur to locate a missing airman in Iran. This AI is said to be able to detect heartbeats and voices from great distances. It is believed to have been used in a desert environment.
insight: Advanced AI can revolutionize surveillance and search operations in challenging environments.

title: Amazon is ending support for these older Kindle and Kindle Fire devices
summary: Amazon is ending support for older Kindle and Kindle Fire devices, starting with models dating back to 2007, including the original Kindle and Kindle Keyboard.
insight: Users will lose access to digital content, updates, and services.

title: Anthropic unveils new powerful AI that finds software flaws, but says it's too dangerous to release
summary: Anthropic's AI, Claude Mythos Preview, discovers software flaws but is deemed too dangerous for public use.
insight: AI vulnerability detection poses risks of widespread exploitation and chaos

title: LG's never-released rollable phone gets torn down, revealing wild engineering
summary: LG's never-released rollable phone has been torn down, revealing its complex engineering. The phone uses a small motor to slide an extra screen out of the side. This design differs from foldable phones like the Galaxy Z Fold.
insight: Innovative rollable design offers a new approach to screen expansion.

title: Motorola returns to tablets with Moto Pad, revamps Moto G Stylus smartphone
summary: Motorola has introduced the Moto Pad, an 11-inch 2.5K tablet with 90Hz display and 5G capabilities.
insight: Motorola's return to the tablet market with a powerful device matters.

title: Apple and Samsung named the least repairable phone brands in new report
summary: Apple and Samsung ranked lowest in repairability scores in a new study. The Failing the Fix 2026 report by the US Public Interest Research Group Education Fund highlights the difficulties of fixing these phones. This study aims to inform consumers about the environmental and financial implications.
insight: E-waste generated by non-repairable phones can harm the environment significantly.

title: MacBook Neo demand is so strong it's creating supply headaches for Apple
summary: Apple's MacBook Neo is in high demand, causing supply chain issues. The laptop features Apple's A18 Pro processor repurposed from iPhone 16 Pro production. This decision may have contributed to the Neo's strong sales.
insight: Repurposed chips drive demand and supply headaches for Apple.

title: Chrome finally gets vertical tabs, plus an improved Reading Mode
summary: Chrome introduces vertical tabs and improved Reading Mode for desktop users, starting April 7. The vertical tab feature allows users to move the tab strip to a sidebar on the left. This feature aims to enhance user experience and productivity.
insight: Streamlined navigation boosts browsing efficiency and user satisfaction

title: NASA just dropped new high-quality Moon photos from Artemis II
summary: NASA has released high-resolution Moon photos from the Artemis II mission. The photos were taken by four astronauts who observed the far side of the Moon during the mission. The images are available for download on NASA's website and Flickr page.
insight: First-ever direct human observations of the Moon's far side

title: Intel is in talks with Google and Amazon to power AI chips with new packaging tech
summary: Intel is partnering with Google and Amazon to leverage new packaging tech for AI chips, shifting focus from back-end manufacturing to system-level design as AI workloads drive demand for specialized dies.
insight: AI workloads require specialized dies stitched together for efficient processing.

title: AMD Ryzen 5 5500X3D Review: AM4 Keeps on Giving
summary: AMD's Ryzen 5 5500X3D brings 3D V-Cache tech to older AM4 sockets, tested in 14 games.
insight: Ryzen 5 5500X3D offers improved gaming performance at an affordable price.
